heading: Residence of retired judges; official station
label: 374 Residence of retired judges; official station
text: Retired judges of the United States are not subject to restrictions as to residence. The place where a retired judge maintains the actual abode in which he customarily lives shall be deemed to be his official station for the purposes of . The place where a judge or magistrate judge recalled under section 155, 375, 636, or 797 of this title maintains the actual abode in which the judge or magistrate judge customarily lives shall be deemed to be the official station of such judge or magistrate judge for purposes of .section 456 of this titlesection 604(a)(7) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 402 (, ).Feb. 11, 1938, ch. 2352 Stat. 28Sections 44 and 133 of this title require that active circuit and district judges shall reside in the circuit or district to which appointed.Changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments1986— inserted last sentence.Pub. L. 99–6511959— inserted sentence to provide that place where retired judge maintains actual abode shall be deemed to be his official station and inserted “; official station” in section catchline.Pub. L. 86–312
Change of NameWords “magistrate judge” substituted for “magistrate” wherever appearing in text pursuant to , set out as a note under .section 321 of Pub. L. 101–650section 631 of this title
Effective Date of 1986 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 99–651section 203 of Pub. L. 99–651section 155 of this titleJan. 1, 1987
